title: haloragis procumbens
intro: Haloragis procumbens, also known as the water milfoil or water dock, is a perennial aquatic plant native to Australia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The plant is rich in flavonoids, glycosides, and saponins, providing various health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestive health, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Haloragis procumbens are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
